name: Mold-Free Rubber Duck Thermometer
desc: A rubber duck that tells you the bath temp — because your elbow apparently can't be trusted. Also claims to be mold-free, which raises questions about all the other ducks.

name: Frida Baby Windi Gas Passer
desc: A tiny tube you insert... where the sun doesn't shine... to release trapped gas. Yes. This is a real product. Yes, parents actually buy this.

name: NoseFrida SnotSucker Nasal Aspirator
desc: You put one end in the baby's nose and the OTHER end in your mouth and suck. There's a filter. They promise. Baby loves it. Parents question their life choices.

name: Bluetooth Pregnancy Belly Headphones
desc: Wrap speakers around your bump so your unborn baby can enjoy Mozart, podcasts, or honestly whatever you're into. Studies on effectiveness are "mixed."

name: Haakaa Manual Silicone Breast Pump
desc: A silicone blob with no moving parts that collects milk purely through suction and vibes. Somehow has 50,000+ 5-star reviews. Parents lose their minds over it.

name: Diaper Genie Complete Pail
desc: A fancy trash can — but make it specifically for poop. Uses proprietary bag refills so you're locked in forever. Smells surprisingly not-terrible.

name: Baby Shusher Sleep Miracle Sound Machine
desc: A hockey-puck-sized device that just shushes your baby over and over, louder than you expected, until they pass out. Desperate parents swear by it.

name: Owlet Dream Sock Baby Monitor
desc: A WiFi-connected sock that monitors your baby's heart rate and oxygen levels while they sleep and sends alerts to your phone. For the parent who needs data on everything.

name: 4moms mamaRoo4 Infant Swing
desc: A space-age baby swing with 5 unique motion settings named things like "car ride" and "kangaroo." Connects to Bluetooth. Costs more than some people's car payment.

name: Frida Baby 3-in-1 Nose, Nail & Ear Picker Set
desc: A full kit dedicated to extracting things from small holes on your baby's face. Comes with special tools, a light, and the confidence of a tiny surgeon.

name: Munchkin Sterilizer Microwave Bags
desc: Throw your bottle parts in a ziplock-looking bag, nuke it for 90 seconds, and call it sterile. Each bag is reusable up to 20 times — because nothing says "parenting" like tracking your bag's use count.

name: Frida Mom Postpartum Recovery Underwear
desc: Hospital-grade mesh underwear sold as a luxury product. Stretchy, enormous, and according to every mother alive, the most comfortable thing they've ever worn. Humbling and iconic.

name: Owlet Cam 2 Smart Baby Monitor
desc: A 1080p HD camera that lets you watch your baby sleep from your phone at 3am instead of just... going back to sleep yourself. Includes night vision and cry detection.

name: Fridababy DermaFrida The SkinSoother
desc: A silicone brush that looks like a tiny waffle iron attachment designed specifically to scrub cradle cap off your baby's head. Sounds aggressive. Parents love it.

name: Summer Infant Comfy Clean Deluxe Bather
desc: A mesh sling that hangs over the sink so you can bathe a newborn with both hands free and zero risk of dropping a slippery wet baby. The name "Comfy Clean" is doing a lot of work here.

name: Ergobaby Omni 360 Baby Carrier
desc: A carrier with four carrying positions including facing out, facing in, hip, and back. Takes 20 minutes to figure out the first time. Then you never take it off.

name: Dr. Brown's Anti-Colic Bottle Warmer
desc: A warmer that uses steam to heat bottles to body temperature in under 4 minutes. Because apparently nobody has time to put a bottle in warm tap water anymore.

name: Nanit Pro Smart Baby Monitor + Breathing Band
desc: A camera that mounts above the crib AND a little band the baby wears on their stomach to detect breathing motion. For parents who want to turn anxiety into a subscription service.

name: Boppy Newborn Lounger
desc: A little foam nest that lets your baby lounge like a tiny Roman emperor while you do dishes. Recalled once, redesigned, still extremely popular. The drama only made it more famous.

name: Water Wipes Original Baby Wipes
desc: Wipes made from 99.9% water and a drop of fruit extract. Yes, just water. In a wipe. Parents pay a premium for this. Dermatologists approve. Economics students weep.

name: Hatch Rest 2nd Gen Sound Machine + Night Light
desc: A smart sound machine and night light that you control from your phone in bed so you never have to get up to adjust the volume. Fully worth it. Completely ridiculous. Both.

name: Tummy Time Water Mat
desc: An inflatable mat filled with water and floating sea creatures designed to make tummy time less miserable for everyone involved. Baby stares at fish. You eat breakfast.

name: Baby Banana Bendable Training Toothbrush
desc: A bright yellow toothbrush shaped like a banana that babies hold and chew on themselves. It's shaped like a banana. That's the whole product. 4.7 stars, 15,000 reviews.

name: Frida Baby 3-in-1 True Temp Thermometer
desc: One thermometer that works in the ear, on the forehead, AND rectally. The box lists all three modes with equal enthusiasm. Comes with a color-coded fever alert.

name: Doona Infant Car Seat & Stroller
desc: A car seat that unfolds into a stroller with the push of a button. No base swap, no transfer. Extremely clever. Also costs roughly as much as a used car.

name: Mustela Foam Shampoo for Newborns
desc: A no-rinse foam shampoo for babies who don't have much hair yet but apparently still need a $14 product for it. Smells incredible. Disappears in two weeks.

name: Skip Hop Explore & More Activity Center
desc: An enormous plastic activity saucer with 26 ways to play, a rotating seat, lights, music, and enough stimulation to mildly overwhelm an adult. Folds flat. Barely.

name: Snoo Smart Sleeper Bassinet
desc: A bassinet that automatically rocks and shushes your baby when it detects crying, using an algorithm. Made by the guy who wrote "Happiest Baby on the Block." Costs more than most people's first car.

name: Copper Pearl Burp Cloths (6-Pack)
desc: Burp cloths that are thick, triple-layered, and actually absorbent unlike the decorative rectangles that come in gift sets. Parents who own these talk about them unprompted at parties.

name: Frida Baby 2-in-1 Postpartum Peri Bottle
desc: A curved squirt bottle for postpartum recovery that is angled so you don't have to contort yourself. Simple concept. Life-changing execution. Every nurse recommends it.

name: Lovevery The Play Gym
desc: A $140 play mat designed by child development experts with specific toys for each developmental stage. Comes with a booklet explaining why each wooden ring matters. Parents feel very seen by this.

name: Dapple Baby Bottle & Dish Soap
desc: Dish soap marketed exclusively for baby items, free of fragrance, dyes, and apparently everything that makes soap fun. Costs 3x regular Dawn. Parents buy it without hesitation.

name: Wooden Baby Gym with Rings & Beads
desc: A minimalist wooden arch with dangly toys that costs significantly more than the plastic version because it matches your Scandinavian nursery aesthetic. Worth it, apparently.

name: Magnetic Me Footie Pajamas
desc: Pajamas that use magnets instead of snaps so you can change a diaper at 3am without accidentally snapping the wrong thing and starting over. Revolutionary. Embarrassingly obvious in hindsight.

name: Baby Einstein Take Along Tunes Musical Toy
desc: A small plastic toy that plays classical music clips on a loop forever, at volumes you cannot adjust, until the battery dies or you hide it under the couch cushions. Babies love it.

name: Wubbanub Pacifier Plush
desc: A pacifier with a small stuffed animal attached so it's harder to lose and easier for baby to grab. The giraffe is the most popular. There is also a manatee. The manatee deserves more attention.

name: Ubbi Steel Diaper Pail
desc: A steel diaper pail with a sliding lid that seals in odors without proprietary bags — takes regular trash bags. Costs more upfront, saves a fortune on refills. The sensible choice.

name: Fridababy MediFrida the Accu-Dose Pacifier
desc: A pacifier with a medicine syringe built into the handle so you can sneak liquid medicine into a baby who refuses it. Devious. Genius. 4.8 stars.

name: Nested Bean Zen Sack Classic
desc: A sleep sack with a small weighted pad over the chest that mimics the feeling of a parent's hand. Babies sleep longer. Parents cry grateful tears into their cold coffee.

name: Skip Hop Moby Waterfall Bath Rinser
desc: A cup with a soft silicone edge that presses against baby's forehead so water runs away from their eyes during rinse. Simple. Effective. Why didn't this exist sooner.

name: Honest Company Diaper & Wipe Bundle
desc: A subscription box of diapers with cute patterns that cost more than regular diapers but arrive at your door and have avocados on them. The avocado print alone accounts for $4.

name: Fisher-Price Deluxe Kick & Play Piano Gym
desc: A play mat where babies kick a piano with their feet and it makes noise. Sounds chaotic. Is chaotic. Baby develops leg muscles and rhythm simultaneously.

name: Munchkin Latch Transition Trainer Sippy Cup
desc: A sippy cup shaped like a breast to ease the transition from nursing. The product exists. The name is "Latch." Everyone at the baby shower will have opinions.

name: Baby Einstein Sea Dreams Soother
desc: A crib attachment with a glowing dome, ocean sounds, and slow-moving sea creatures that plays for 30 minutes. Baby watches fish. You eat dinner with two hands.

name: Infantino Flip 4-in-1 Convertible Carrier
desc: A convertible baby carrier for newborns through toddlers at a fraction of the Ergobaby price. Does 95% of the same job. The other 5% is a logo.

name: aden + anais Classic Muslin Swaddle Blankets (4-Pack)
desc: Extremely thin cotton blankets that somehow every hospital nurse uses to swaddle babies. You will own twelve of these by the end of the first month and still need more.

name: Graco Simple Sway Swing
desc: A plug-in baby swing with 6 speeds and nature sounds that does not require D batteries, which is the single most important feature and not mentioned until page 4 of the manual.

name: Kiinde Twist Breastmilk Storage Pouches (20-Pack)
desc: Pre-sterilized pouches that attach directly to a breast pump and then to a bottle with zero transfer. The whole system is one continuous sealed loop. Engineers designed this. It shows.

name: Nuby Nite Nite Lullaby Light & Sound Soother
desc: A plush elephant that plays 8 lullabies and glows softly for 18 minutes then shuts off automatically. Has a star projector. Costs less than a dinner out. Does more than a dinner out.

name: Summer Infant Pop 'N Sit Portable Booster
desc: A booster seat that folds flat like a pop-up hamper and fits in a diaper bag. For restaurants, grandma's house, camping, wherever. Parents who own this mention it constantly.
